reasoning: The event requires the U.S. government to publicly accuse Russia of invading any country other than Ukraine before July 1, 2025. This has not occurred yet (as of May 1, 2025). The timeline until the resolution date is extremely short (only two months). A new ground invasion by Russia is highly unlikely right now given its heavy military commitment and attrition in Ukraine. Furthermore, recent articles indicate that the U.S. (under the Trump administration in early 2025) has shifted towards a more conciliatory stance regarding Russia (even siding with Russia on a UN war resolution in Feb 2025). Therefore, the U.S. is very unlikely to proactively accuse Russia of a new invasion unless a glaring, undeniable military attack occurs. The absence of any reported troop buildups near countries like Moldova, Georgia, or the Baltic states provides strong evidence that no new invasion is imminent. Combining the very low base rate of such an occurrence within a two-month span with the current U.S. political orientation forms a solid basis for predicting practically a 2% chance.
